name: Trobec's School Bus Svc
principals: Tim Schubert (former CEO, sold to daughters 2025)
recent_news: July 2025: Tim Schubert sold the business to his daughters Becca Atkins and Bethany Bertram, who became co-owners; multi-generational family transition continues.
fit_notes: Long-tenured Central MN family operator (87 years) serving St. Cloud/Sartell districts plus motorcoach charters; succession just happened to next-gen daughters in 2025 so near-term sale is less likely, but strong tuck-in candidate for MN footprint if approached in 3-5 years.

name: Safe Journey 2 School
owner_or_parent: independent (managed by WEDOMORE LLC per company website)
principals: Attiya S. Jones (Owner, per source data)
recent_news: No notable public news found; small minority/women-owned looking operator with limited online footprint.
fit_notes: Small Chicago southside operator (~80 employees per source) with very thin public footprint; possible MWBE/diversity vendor for CPS. Too small and opaque for confident diligence without direct outreach; could be a low-priority tuck-in if it carries CPS or charter-school contracts.

name: Albert Lea Bus Co Inc
principals: Note: source data lists 'Steve Anderson (Director)' — that is the Albert Lea Area Schools Director of Facilities & Transportation, NOT the bus company owner
recent_news: No notable recent M&A or news; company describes itself as 30+ year family-owned local operator; partner of Riverland Community College for CDL training pipeline.
fit_notes: Classic southern MN family operator with sole/anchor contract for Albert Lea schools; 30+ year history suggests aging principal and likely succession opportunity in next 3-7 years; small but clean platform-add for a MN-anchored Summit roll-up.

name: Interntonal SCHL Bus Svc Isbs
recent_news: No public news; no functional company website; the 'globayo.com' URL in source data appears to be a generic/unrelated domain, not the operator's site. No Illinois Secretary of State or SAFER hit confirmed for an 'International School Bus Service' at 3258 W 85th Pl, Chicago.
fit_notes: Very small (22 employees) Chicago southwest-side operator with effectively no online footprint; could be a single-owner SPED/contract carrier or a defunct/renamed entity. Not actionable as a tuck-in without phone outreach to confirm it still operates.

name: Illinois School Bus Co
owner_or_parent: Likely Cook-Illinois Corporation (subsidiary 'Illinois School Bus Company, Inc.') — Cook-Illinois announced sale to First Student Inc. (Feb 2026). NOTE: Cook-Illinois's primary Illinois School Bus Co. facility is at 13939 S. Cicero Ave, Crestwood, IL 60445, not Lansing — the 17714 Locust St, Lansing IL address may be a separate/yard location or a misattribution in source data.
principals: John Benish Jr. (Cook-Illinois Corp CEO, pre-First Student deal)
districts_or_customers: Twelve south Cook County districts (per Cook-Illinois)
recent_news: Feb 2026: Cook-Illinois Corporation's Kickert subsidiary sold to First Student Inc. of Cincinnati; Cook-Illinois broadly is described as 'a First Student company' on its 2026 website, signaling parent-level transaction with First Student (TransLink/EQT).
website: https://cookillinois.com/illinois-school-bus-company-inc/ (source data lists 'army.mil' which is clearly wrong)
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in candidate — parent Cook-Illinois has been acquired by/aligned with First Student (EQT-backed), the largest US school-bus operator and a direct Summit competitor. Address mismatch (Lansing vs. Crestwood) should be reconciled but the underlying entity is no longer independently for sale.
